SapModel.File.NewBlank.parameter_notes: No parameters. Call InitializeNewModel() before this.
SapModel.File.NewBlank.notes: Returns ret_code directly (0=success). No ByRef outputs.

SapModel.PropMaterial.SetMaterial.description: Define or modify a material by name and type
SapModel.PropMaterial.SetMaterial.parameter_notes: Name: str; MatType: int (1=Steel, 2=Concrete, 3=NoDesign, 4=Aluminum, 5=ColdFormed, 6=Rebar, 7=Tendon); Color: int (-1=default); Notes: str; GUID: str
SapModel.PropMaterial.SetMaterial.notes: Returns ret_code directly (0=success). No ByRef outputs. Minimum required: Name, MatType.

SapModel.PropMaterial.SetMPIsotropic.description: Set isotropic mechanical properties (E, Poisson, thermal coeff)
SapModel.PropMaterial.SetMPIsotropic.parameter_notes: Name: str (existing material); E: float (modulus of elasticity [F/L^2]); U: float (Poisson ratio); A: float (thermal expansion coeff [1/T]); Temp: float (default=0)
SapModel.PropMaterial.SetMPIsotropic.notes: Prerequisites: material must exist via SetMaterial. Returns ret_code (0=success).

SapModel.PropMaterial.SetWeightAndMass.description: Set weight per unit volume and mass per unit volume
SapModel.PropMaterial.SetWeightAndMass.parameter_notes: Name: str (existing material); MyType: int (1=weight per unit volume [F/L^3], 2=mass per unit volume [M/L^3]); Value: float; Temp: float (default=0)
SapModel.PropMaterial.SetWeightAndMass.notes: Prerequisites: material must exist via SetMaterial. Returns ret_code (0=success). Call twice — once for weight (MyType=1) and once for mass (MyType=2).

SapModel.PropFrame.SetRectangle.signature: (Name, MatProp, T3, T2, Color, Notes, GUID) → ret_code
SapModel.PropFrame.SetRectangle.parameter_notes: Name: str; MatProp: str (existing material); T3: float (depth/height [L]); T2: float (width [L]); Color: int (-1=default); Notes: str; GUID: str
SapModel.PropFrame.SetRectangle.notes: Returns ret_code directly (0=success). Common for concrete beams and columns.

SapModel.PropFrame.SetCircle.description: Define a solid circular frame section
SapModel.PropFrame.SetCircle.signature: (Name, MatProp, T3, Color, Notes, GUID) → ret_code
SapModel.PropFrame.SetCircle.parameter_notes: Name: str; MatProp: str (existing material); T3: float (diameter [L]); Color: int (-1=default); Notes: str; GUID: str
SapModel.PropFrame.SetCircle.notes: Returns ret_code directly (0=success). Common for piles, drilled shafts, and circular columns.

SapModel.PropFrame.SetISection.signature: (Name, MatProp, T3, T2, TF, TW, T2B, TFB, Color, Notes, GUID) → ret_code
SapModel.PropFrame.SetISection.parameter_notes: Name: str; MatProp: str; T3: float (overall depth); T2: float (top flange width); TF: float (top flange thickness); TW: float (web thickness); T2B: float (bottom flange width); TFB: float (bottom flange thickness); Color: int; Notes: str; GUID: str
SapModel.PropFrame.SetISection.notes: Supports asymmetric I-sections (different top/bottom flanges). For symmetric use equal T2=T2B and TF=TFB.

SapModel.PropFrame.SetTube.description: Define a rectangular tube (HSS) frame section
SapModel.PropFrame.SetTube.signature: (Name, MatProp, T3, T2, TF, TW, Color, Notes, GUID) → ret_code
SapModel.PropFrame.SetTube.parameter_notes: Name: str; MatProp: str; T3: float (outer depth [L]); T2: float (outer width [L]); TF: float (flange/top+bottom wall thickness [L]); TW: float (web/left+right wall thickness [L]); Color: int; Notes: str; GUID: str
SapModel.PropFrame.SetTube.notes: Returns ret_code directly (0=success). Common for HSS braces, columns, and truss elements.

SapModel.PointObj.AddCartesian.description: Create a point object at Cartesian coordinates

SapModel.FrameObj.AddByPoint.description: Create a frame element between two existing points

SapModel.FrameObj.AddByCoord.description: Add a frame element defined by endpoint coordinates

SapModel.FrameObj.SetSection.description: Assign or change the section property of a frame element

SapModel.PropArea.SetShell_1.description: Create a shell (area) section property with uniform thickness

SapModel.AreaObj.AddByCoord.description: Create an area element from corner coordinates

SapModel.AreaObj.SetSpring.description: Assign area springs (Winkler foundation) to an area element

SapModel.LoadPatterns.Add.description: Add a new load pattern to the model
SapModel.LoadPatterns.Add.notes: Returns ret_code directly (0=success). No ByRef outputs. signature: (Name, MyType, SelfWTMultiplier, AddCase) → ret_code

SapModel.Func.FuncRS.SetUser.description: Define a user-defined response spectrum function from period-value pairs
SapModel.Func.FuncRS.SetUser.notes: Returns ByRef outputs (Period[], Value[], ret_code) — extract last element as ret_code. DampRatio=0.05 for 5% damping.

SapModel.LoadCases.ResponseSpectrum.SetL.description: Assign directional response spectrum loads to a spectrum load case
SapModel.LoadCases.ResponseSpectrum.SetL.notes: Returns ByRef outputs (LoadName[], Func[], SF[], CSys[], Ang[], ret_code) — extract last element as ret_code. Prerequisites: RS function and RS load case must exist.

SapModel.RespCombo.Add.description: Create a new response combination (linear, envelope, SRSS, etc.)

SapModel.RespCombo.SetCaseList.description: Add a load case to a response combination with scale factor

SapModel.RespCombo.GetCaseList.description: Query the load cases and scale factors in a combination

SapModel.EditArea.Divide.description: Divide an area object into a mesh of smaller areas

SapModel.SelectObj.CoordinateRange.description: Select objects within a coordinate bounding box

SapModel.ConstraintDef.SetBody.description: Define a body constraint (rigid link) for DOF coupling

SapModel.FrameObj.SetTCLimits.description: Set tension/compression force limits for a frame element

SapModel.PointObj.SetRestraint.description: Assign translational and rotational restraints to a joint

SapModel.FrameObj.SetLoadDistributed.description: Apply uniform/trapezoidal distributed loads on frames
SapModel.FrameObj.SetLoadDistributed.parameter_notes: Name: str; LoadPat: str; MyType: int (1=Force/L, 2=Moment/L); Dir: int (10=Gravity, 6=Z, etc.); Dist1/Dist2: float (relative 0-1 or absolute); Val1/Val2: float [F/L]; CSys: str ('Global'); RelDist: bool (True); Replace: bool (True); ItemType: int (0)
SapModel.FrameObj.SetLoadDistributed.notes: For uniform load set Val1=Val2. For trapezoidal use different Val1/Val2. Dir=10 for gravity. Returns ret_code directly (int, 0=success).

SapModel.PointObj.SetLoadForce.description: Apply forces/moments at a joint (6 DOF)
SapModel.PointObj.SetLoadForce.parameter_notes: Name: str (point name); LoadPat: str (load pattern); Value: float[6] [F1,F2,F3,M1,M2,M3]; Replace: bool (True); CSys: str ('Global'); ItemType: int (0=Object). Returns [Value_byref, ret_code] — check ret[-1]==0 for success.
SapModel.PointObj.SetLoadForce.notes: Returns [Value_byref, ret_code] — NOT a plain int. Check raw_sf[-1]==0. Value array in coordinate system directions.

SapModel.Results.JointDispl.parameter_notes: Name: str (specific joint name — 'All' not supported); ItemTypeElm: int (0=Object); 12 ByRef: NumberResults, Obj[], Elm[], LoadCase[], StepType[], StepNum[], U1[], U2[], U3[], R1[], R2[], R3[]
SapModel.Results.JointDispl.notes: Call Results.Setup.SetCaseSelectedForOutput first. ret_code=raw[-1], U1=raw[6], U2=raw[7], U3=raw[8], R1=raw[9], R2=raw[10], R3=raw[11]. IMPORTANT: 'All' does NOT work as a wildcard — pass specific joint names only.

SapModel.Results.FrameForce.description: Extract frame element internal forces (P, V2, V3, T, M2, M3)
SapModel.Results.FrameForce.parameter_notes: Name: str (frame name or specific name — 'All' not supported); ItemTypeElm: int (0=Object); 14 ByRef arrays returned: NumberResults, Obj[], ObjSta[], Elm[], ElmSta[], LoadCase[], StepType[], StepNum[], P[], V2[], V3[], T[], M2[], M3[]
SapModel.Results.FrameForce.notes: Call Results.Setup.SetCaseSelectedForOutput before extracting. ret_code=raw[-1], num_results=raw[0], P=raw[8], V2=raw[9], V3=raw[10], T=raw[11], M2=raw[12], M3=raw[13]. Model must be saved and analyzed first.

SapModel.Results.JointReact.parameter_notes: Name: str (specific joint name — 'All' not supported); ItemTypeElm: int (0=Object); 12 ByRef: NumberResults, Obj[], Elm[], LoadCase[], StepType[], StepNum[], F1[], F2[], F3[], M1[], M2[], M3[]
SapModel.Results.JointReact.notes: Call Results.Setup.SetCaseSelectedForOutput first. ret_code=raw[-1], F1=raw[6], F2=raw[7], F3=raw[8], M1=raw[9], M2=raw[10], M3=raw[11]. IMPORTANT: 'All' does NOT work as a wildcard — pass specific joint names only.

SapModel.File.OpenFile.description: Open an existing .sdb model file by path
SapModel.File.OpenFile.parameter_notes: FileName: str (full path to .sdb, .$2k, .s2k, .xlsx, .xls, or .mdb file)
SapModel.File.OpenFile.notes: Returns ret_code directly (0=success). No ByRef outputs. Only use from external API — not VBA inside SAP2000.

SapModel.File.New2DFrame.description: Create a 2D portal/braced frame from parametric template
SapModel.File.New2DFrame.parameter_notes: TempType: int (0=PortalFrame, 1=ConcentricBraced, 2=EccentricBraced); NumberStorys: int; StoryHeight: float [L]; NumberBays: int; BayWidth: float [L]; Restraint: bool (default=True); Beam: str ('Default' or section name); Column: str ('Default' or section name); Brace: str ('Default' or section name, portal only)
SapModel.File.New2DFrame.notes: Creates new model — do not use to add to existing model. Call InitializeNewModel() first.

SapModel.File.New3DFrame.description: Create a 3D building frame from parametric template
SapModel.File.New3DFrame.parameter_notes: TempType: int (0=OpenFrame, 1=PerimeterFrame, 2=BeamSlab, 3=FlatPlate); NumberStorys: int; StoryHeight: float [L]; NumberBaysX: int; BayWidthX: float [L]; NumberBaysY: int; BayWidthY: float [L]; Restraint: bool (True); Beam/Column/Area: str ('Default'); NumberXDivisions/NumberYDivisions: int (4, for BeamSlab/FlatPlate)
SapModel.File.New3DFrame.notes: Creates new model — do not use to add to existing model. Area/Divisions only apply to BeamSlab and FlatPlate types.

SapModel.File.NewBeam.parameter_notes: NumberSpans: int; SpanLength: float [L]; Restraint: bool (default=True); Beam: str ('Default' or section name)
SapModel.File.NewBeam.notes: Creates new model — do not use to add to existing model. Restraints at each span end.

SapModel.File.NewWall.description: Create a shear wall template from area element grid
SapModel.File.NewWall.parameter_notes: NumberXDivisions: int; DivisionWidthX: float [L]; NumberZDivisions: int; DivisionWidthZ: float [L]; Restraint: bool (default=True); Area: str ('Default' or shell section name)
SapModel.File.NewWall.notes: Creates new model — do not use to add to existing model. Wall is built as a grid of area elements.

SapModel.FrameObj.SetLoadPoint.description: Apply a concentrated point load along a frame element
SapModel.FrameObj.SetLoadPoint.parameter_notes: Name: str; LoadPat: str; MyType: int (1=Force, 2=Moment); Dir: int (10=Gravity, etc.); Dist: float (0-1 relative or absolute); Val: float [F] or [FL]; CSys: str ('Global'); RelDist: bool (True); Replace: bool (True); ItemType: int (0)
SapModel.FrameObj.SetLoadPoint.notes: Returns ret_code directly (int, 0=success). Set Replace=False to add multiple point loads to the same frame.

SapModel.AreaObj.SetLoadUniform.description: Apply uniform surface pressure on area elements (slab loads)
SapModel.AreaObj.SetLoadUniform.parameter_notes: Name: str; LoadPat: str; Value: float [F/L^2]; Dir: int (10=Gravity, 6=Z, etc.); Replace: bool (True); CSys: str ('Global'); ItemType: int (0)
SapModel.AreaObj.SetLoadUniform.notes: Returns ret_code directly (int, 0=success). Negative value for downward pressure with gravity direction (Dir=10).

SapModel.AreaObj.SetLoadGravity.description: Apply gravity multiplier on area element self-weight
SapModel.AreaObj.SetLoadGravity.parameter_notes: Name: str; LoadPat: str; x/y/z: float (gravity multipliers in global directions); Replace: bool (True); CSys: str ('Global'); ItemType: int (0)
SapModel.AreaObj.SetLoadGravity.notes: Returns ret_code directly (int, 0=success). Use z=-1 for standard 1g downward gravity. Material must have weight/mass defined for this to produce forces.

SapModel.FrameObj.SetLoadTemperature.description: Apply thermal loads on frame elements
SapModel.FrameObj.SetLoadTemperature.parameter_notes: Name: str; LoadPat: str; MyType: int (1=Temperature, 2=Gradient2-2, 3=Gradient3-3); Val: float [T] or [T/L]; PatternName: str (''=none); Replace: bool (True); ItemType: int (0)
SapModel.FrameObj.SetLoadTemperature.notes: Returns ret_code directly (int, 0=success). Requires material with thermal expansion coefficient defined (SetMPIsotropic with non-zero A). PatternName can be omitted (defaults to empty string).

SapModel.PropFrame.SetPipe.description: Define a circular hollow section (pipe/HSS round)
SapModel.PropFrame.SetPipe.parameter_notes: Name: str; MatProp: str; T3: float (outer diameter [L]); TW: float (wall thickness [L]); Color: int (-1=default); Notes: str; GUID: str
SapModel.PropFrame.SetPipe.notes: Returns ret_code directly (0=success). Common for CHS columns, braces, and truss elements.

SapModel.PropFrame.SetChannel.parameter_notes: Name: str; MatProp: str; T3: float (overall depth [L]); T2: float (flange width [L]); TF: float (flange thickness [L]); TW: float (web thickness [L]); Color: int; Notes: str; GUID: str
SapModel.PropFrame.SetChannel.notes: Returns ret_code directly (0=success). Common for purlins, girts, light structural members.

SapModel.PropFrame.SetAngle.parameter_notes: Name: str; MatProp: str; T3: float (vertical leg depth [L]); T2: float (horizontal leg width [L]); TF: float (horizontal leg thickness [L]); TW: float (vertical leg thickness [L]); Color: int; Notes: str; GUID: str
SapModel.PropFrame.SetAngle.notes: Returns ret_code directly (0=success). Supports equal and unequal angles.

SapModel.PropFrame.SetTee.parameter_notes: Name: str; MatProp: str; T3: float (overall depth [L]); T2: float (flange width [L]); TF: float (flange thickness [L]); TW: float (stem thickness [L]); Color: int; Notes: str; GUID: str
SapModel.PropFrame.SetTee.notes: Returns ret_code directly (0=success). Common for WT shapes (cut I-sections) and composite construction.

SapModel.FrameObj.SetReleases.description: Set end releases (moment releases for hinges)
SapModel.FrameObj.SetReleases.parameter_notes: Name: str; ii: bool[6] i-end [P,V2,V3,T,M2,M3] True=released; jj: bool[6] j-end; StartValue: float[6] partial fixity springs at i-end (0=fully released); EndValue: float[6] at j-end; ItemType: int (0)
SapModel.FrameObj.SetReleases.notes: Returns ByRef tuple: (ii_out, jj_out, start_out, end_out, ret_code). Use raw[-1] to check success. For simple pin, release M3=[False,False,False,False,False,True]. Use StartValue/EndValue=0.0 for fully released.

SapModel.FrameObj.SetEndLengthOffset.description: Set rigid-zone offsets at beam-column joints
SapModel.FrameObj.SetEndLengthOffset.parameter_notes: Name: str; AutoOffset: bool (True=auto from connected objects); Length1: float (i-end offset [L]); Length2: float (j-end offset [L]); RzFactor: float (0-1, rigid-zone factor, 1=fully rigid, 0.5=half-rigid); ItemType: int (0)
SapModel.FrameObj.SetEndLengthOffset.notes: Returns ret_code directly (0=success). Length1/Length2 ignored when AutoOffset=True.

SapModel.PropFrame.SetModifiers.parameter_notes: Name: str (section name); Value: float[8] [Area, AS2, AS3, Torsion, I22, I33, Mass, Weight] — 1.0=no mod, 0.35=ACI beam, 0.70=ACI column
SapModel.PropFrame.SetModifiers.notes: Returns ByRef tuple: (value_out, ret_code). Use raw[-1] to check success. ACI 318: beams 0.35*Ig, columns 0.70*Ig. Array has 8 elements.

SapModel.DesignSteel.SetCode.description: Set the steel design code for the model
SapModel.DesignSteel.SetCode.notes: Returns ret_code (0=success). Use GetCode to verify. Pair with: DesignSteel.StartDesign.

SapModel.DesignSteel.StartDesign.parameter_notes: No parameters. Model must be analyzed first and steel code set.
SapModel.DesignSteel.StartDesign.notes: Returns ret_code (0=success). Requires Analyze.RunAnalysis first. Set code with DesignSteel.SetCode before calling. Model must be saved (File.Save) before RunAnalysis.

SapModel.DesignConcrete.StartDesign.parameter_notes: No parameters. Model must be analyzed first and concrete code set.
SapModel.DesignConcrete.StartDesign.notes: Returns ret_code (0=success). Requires Analyze.RunAnalysis first. Set code with DesignConcrete.SetCode before calling. Model must be saved (File.Save) before RunAnalysis.

SapModel.Results.AreaForceShell.description: Extract shell element forces and moments
SapModel.Results.AreaForceShell.parameter_notes: Name: str (specific area name — 'All' not supported); ItemTypeElm: int (0=Object); 25 ByRef outputs including membrane forces (F11,F22,F12), bending moments (M11,M22,M12), shear (V13,V23), and principal values (FMax,FMin,MMax,MMin,VMax)
SapModel.Results.AreaForceShell.notes: Call Results.Setup.SetCaseSelectedForOutput first. ret_code=raw[-1], num_results=raw[0]. Results at each corner of each element. AreaObj.AddByCoord returns [X_coords, Y_coords, Z_coords, Name_str, ret_code] — use raw[3] for area name. 'All' not supported as wildcard — use specific area name.

SapModel.GroupDef.SetGroup.description: Create or modify a group definition with all flags
SapModel.GroupDef.SetGroup.parameter_notes: Name: str; Color: int (-1=default); 12 bool flags for selection, section cuts, design contexts, mass/weight, etc. All flags default True except AutoSeismicOutput and AutoWindOutput (default False).
SapModel.GroupDef.SetGroup.notes: IMPORTANT: COM bridge exposes as SetGroup (NOT SetGroup_1). Returns ret_code (0=success). 'ALL' group always exists by default. Use FrameObj.SetGroupAssign / AreaObj.SetGroupAssign to add objects to group.

SapModel.GroupDef.GetNameList.description: Get list of all defined group names
SapModel.GroupDef.GetNameList.parameter_notes: NumberNames: int (pass 0, ByRef); MyName: list (pass [], ByRef returns str[])
SapModel.GroupDef.GetNameList.notes: Returns ret_code=raw[-1], count=raw[0], names=raw[1]. Default 'ALL' group (uppercase) always present. Call with GetNameList(0, []).

SapModel.GroupDef.GetAssignments.description: Get objects assigned to a group
SapModel.GroupDef.GetAssignments.parameter_notes: Name: str (group name); NumberItems: int (pass 0, ByRef); ObjectType: list (ByRef int[]); ObjectName: list (ByRef str[])
SapModel.GroupDef.GetAssignments.notes: Returns ret_code=raw[-1], count=raw[0], types=raw[1], names=raw[2]. Object type codes: 1=Point, 2=Frame, 3=Cable, 4=Tendon, 5=Area, 6=Solid, 7=Link. Use SetGroupAssign on object types to populate groups.

SapModel.SourceMass.SetDefault.description: Set the default mass source for analysis
SapModel.SourceMass.SetDefault.parameter_notes: Name: str — mass source name to set as default
SapModel.SourceMass.SetDefault.notes: IMPORTANT: API path is SapModel.SourceMass (NOT SapModel.MassSource). Use SetMassSource to define mass sources before setting default. SetMassSource returns [LoadPat_byref, SF_byref, ret_code] — use raw[-1]. Use GetDefault to verify.

SapModel.DatabaseTables.GetTableForDispl.description: Extract data from SAP2000 display tables as 1D array
SapModel.DatabaseTables.GetTableForDispl.parameter_notes: TableKey: str (e.g., 'Joint Coordinates'); FieldKeyList: list [] (not str); GroupName: str ('' for all); TableVersion: int 0 (ByRef Integer); FieldKeysIncluded: [] (ByRef); NumberRecords: 0 (ByRef); TableData: [] (ByRef)
SapModel.DatabaseTables.GetTableForDispl.notes: CRITICAL: TableVersion must be integer 0 (not string ""). FieldKeyList must be [] (not ""). Return layout: raw[0]=FieldKeyList_byref, raw[1]=TableVersion(int), raw[2]=FieldKeysIncluded(tuple), raw[3]=NumberRecords(int), raw[4]=TableData(tuple), raw[-1]=ret_code. Data is 1D row-major: parse rows with tableData[i*num_cols:(i+1)*num_cols]. Use GetAllTables to discover available TableKeys.

SapModel.PointObj.SetMass.description: Assign concentrated (lumped) mass to a joint
SapModel.PointObj.SetMass.parameter_notes: Name: str; Value: float[6] [M_UX, M_UY, M_UZ, M_RX, M_RY, M_RZ] in mass units (kg for kN_m_C)
SapModel.PointObj.SetMass.notes: IMPORTANT: COM bridge exposes as SetMass (NOT SetLoadMass). No LoadPat, Replace, CSys, or ItemType args — directly sets structural mass. Returns [Value_byref(tuple), ret_code]. Use GetMass(Name, [0]*6) to verify. For kN_m_C units, mass is in kg. SetMass completely replaces existing mass values.
